title: astragalus argophyllus var. alvius
intro: Astragalus argophyllus var. alvius, also known as Alvus milkvetch, is a plant species in the legume family. It is primarily found in the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immune-boosting effects. Astragalus argophyllus var. alvius is often prepared as a dried root, tea, or capsule, and its health benefits may include reducing stress, improving sleep, and supporting the immune system.
